title: Picklum and Robinson claim 2025 Lexus Tahiti Pro crowns
description: SURFING NEWS - Australians Molly Picklum and...
bodyText:
SURFING NEWS - Australians Molly Picklum and Jack Robinson have taken top honours at the Lexus Tahiti Pro Presented by I-SEA, the final regular-season stop on the 2025 World Surf League Championship Tour.


> The iconic “End of the Road” delivered powerful six-to-eight-foot barrels as the event locked in the men’s and women’s Final 5, crowned the Rookies of the Year, and produced standout performances.


Picklum unstoppable in maiden Tahiti win

Picklum dominated the women’s final against reigning World Champion Caity Simmers (USA), posting 17.26 to Simmers’ 4.94 – one of the highest totals of the season.

The 22-year-old’s win cements her No. 1 ranking heading into the Lexus WSL Finals in Fiji later this month.

"What a special place, Tahiti. It’s where the dream comes alive. I finished number one this year – a massive step in the right direction," she said.

Robinson delivers dream finish

Jack Robinson produced a fairytale result, winning both the men’s event and a place in the Final 5. His ninth CT win saw him overcome close friend Griffin Colapinto (USA) in the final, highlighted by a 9.50 barrel.

"I had a mission and went for it. Blessed to be going to Fiji – it comes down to the last one and we’re right there again," Robinson said.

Molly Picklum of Australia surfs in the Final at the Lexus Tahiti Pro on 13 August. Photo: Beatriz Ryder/World Surf League

Final 5 set for Fiji

Men: Yago Dora (BRA), Jordy Smith (RSA), Griffin Colapinto (USA), Jack Robinson (AUS), Italo Ferreira (BRA).Women: Molly Picklum (AUS) leads the pack, with Caity Simmers (USA) seeded third.

Rookies of the Year

Canadian Erin Brooks and Frenchman Marco Mignot took the 2025 Rookie of the Year honours after strong debut seasons.Women’s Final:

Molly Picklum (AUS) – 17.26Caity Simmers (USA) – 4.94Men’s Final:

Jack Robinson (AUS) – 16.90Griffin Colapinto (USA) – 13.67

The Lexus WSL Finals Fiji will run from 27 August to 4 September at Cloudbreak in a one-day, winner-takes-all format.

Jack Robinson of Australia surfs in the Final at the Lexus Tahiti Pro. Photo: Brent Bielmann/World Surf LeagueFull results: worldsurfleague.com

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
